properties.remark: MULTNOMAH COUNTY LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED LANDSLIDE ON THE COLUMBIA RIVER HISTORIC HIGHWAY NEAR THE STARK STREET BRIDGE.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED A LANDSLIDE THAT CLOSED BOTH LANES OF HIGHWAY 224 NEAR TONG ROAD IN DAMASCUS.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED A LANDSLIDE NEAR SKYLINE BOULEVARD AND BURNSIDE. APROXIMATELY 600 CUBIC YARDS OF DEBRIS SLID ONTO THE ROADWAY.
KOIN TV REPORTS LANDSLIDE CLOSING WA HIGHWAY 4 JUST NORTH OF SKAMOKAWA NEAR WOLD RD.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED A LANDSLIDE ON US HIGHWAY 26 NEAR THE VISTA RIDGE TUNNEL THAT BLOCKED TWO EASTBOUND LANES.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED A LANDSLIDE NEAR NORTHWEST THOMPSON ROAD AND CORNELL ROAD THAT BLOCKED LANES IN BOTH DIRECTIONS.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSE A LANDSLIDE ON HIGHWAY 6 AT MILEPOST 20, EAST OF TILLAMOOK, OR. BOTH LANES ARE BLOCKED BY THE LANDSLIDE.
MULTIPLE LANDSLIDES ALONG HIGHWAY 47 BETWEEN BURNS ROAD AND APIARY ROAD.
LANDSIDE BLOCKED I-5 NORTHBOUND NEAR MP 23 AND DIKE ROAD. THERE ARE REPORTS THAT ONE VEHICLE WAS HIT BY THE SLIDE, BUT NO INJURIES RESULTED.
WATERSPOUT OBSERVED OFFSHORE OF LINCOLN CITY. VERIFIED BY PHOTO.

AFTER REPORTS OF DAMAGE IN THE COVINGTON AND THE ORCHARDS AREA OF VANCOUVER, WA, REPORTED BY AN ACQUAINTANCE OF AN NWS EMPLOYEE AND FROM THE NEWS MEDIA, NWS EMPLOYEES DETERMINED A WEAK EF0 TORNADO LIKELY CAUSED THE DAMAGE.
TREES AND BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN IN VICINITY OF ONEONTA GORGE AND HORSETAIL FALLS TRAILS INJURED A FEW HIKERS. EMERGENCY OFFICIALS RESPONDED TO ONE INJURED HIKER WITH POSSIBLE BROKEN ARM.
HAIL OBSERVED 5 MILES NNW OF CARSON, OR
MUD AND DEBRIS 4 TO 6 INCHES DEEP AND 150 FEET WIDE OVER FRONTAGE ROAD / HWY 30 DIRECTLY ADJACENT TO I-84 MP36 CAUSED BY RUNOFF FROM HEAVY RAIN NEAR DODSON AND WARRENDALE
PICTURE OF HAIL IN EXCESS OF 1 INCH IN DIAMETER.
WINDS BROUGHT DOWN POWER LINES, AND WITH TREES SMOKING. THIS ALONG HIGHWAY 47, BETWEEN TOP HILL AND JOHNSON ROAD.
HAIL REPORT RELAYED VIA EUGENE PUBLIC WORKS. ESTIMATED SIZE OF HAIL OBSERVED BY EMPLOYEE.
WATERSPOUT OFFSHORE, DISSIPATED AS MOVE ASHORE AT HAPPY CAMP. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
A RESIDENT NEAR ROSBURG REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL.

A SHORT-LIVED EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS IN THE RANGE OF 65 TO 75 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ALONG GREEN MOUNTAIN DRIVE EAST-NORTHEAST OF LACOMB. ESTIMATED PATH WIDTH 500 FEET, LENGTH 1200 FEET. FIVE FARM BUILDINGS DAMAGED,MAINLY TO THE METAL AND FABRIC ROOFS,ALONG WITH A COUPLE OF BROKEN WOODEN TRUSSES. TWO POWER POLES DOWNED, AND SEVERAL TREE LIMBS DOWN.

UPDATED THE EF RATING TO EF1. THIS IS BASED ON ADDITIONAL SURVEY INFORMATION COLLECTED SEPTEMBER 20TH, THAT EXTENDED THE TRACK EAST, TO A TOTAL LENGTH OF 1 MILE. THIS IS BASED PRIMARILY ON TREE DAMAGE ON THE EASTERN HALF OF THE TRACK, THAT INCLUDED 6 FIR TREES FROM 4 TO 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER THAT WERE SNAPPED. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS ARE 86 TO 105 MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG GREEN MOUNTAIN DRIVE. ESTIMATED PATH WIDTH 500 FEET. IN ADDITION TO TREE DAMAGE, FIVE FARM BUILDINGS DAMAGED AND TWO POWER POLES WERE DOWNED.
PACIFIC COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RELAYED A REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT LOCATED JUST OFFSHORE OF THE SEAVIEW BEACH APPROACH. DISTANCE OFFSHORE ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SPOTTER REPORT.
THREE WATERSPOUTS OBSERVED SIMULTANEOUSLY.

PUBLIC REPORT OF DAMAGE TO TREES, A FENCE, AND PATIO FURNITURE. PHOTOS CONFIRM THAT A SECTION OF A FENCE WAS KNOCKED OVER AND SOME TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED. UNABLE TO CONFIRM WAS DUE TO A TORNADO, BUT UNCONFIRMED FUNNEL CLOUD WAS REPORTED IN SAME LOCATION.
NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WILL BE DETAILED IN A PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT (PNS).

EFO TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ON AND IN THE VICINITY OF THE NORTH END OF THE AURORA AIRPORT. TWO AIRPLANES WERE FLIPPED OVER. MINOR DAMAGE TO A BUILDING ON AIRPORT PROPERTY. DAMAGE AT A NURSERY IMMEDIATELY WEST OF THE AIRPORT INCLUDED DAMAGE TO GREENHOUSES. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED 65 TO 70 MPH. THIS IS AN UPDATE TO PREVIOUS STORM REPORT WHICH REFERENCED BARLOW OREGON.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF A TORNADO NEAR THE AURORA AIRPORT INCLUDING THE AURORA AIRPORT OBSERVER.
SEVERAL TREE LIMBS DOWN. ONE VEHICLE DAMAGED BY A FALLEN LIMB.

EFO TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ON AND IN THE VICINITY OF THE NORTH END OF THE AURORA AIRPORT. TWO AIRPLANES WERE FLIPPED OVER. MINOR DAMAGE TO A BUILDING ON AIRPORT PROPERTY. DAMAGE AT A NURSERY IMMEDIATELY WEST OF THE AIRPORT INCLUDED DAMAGE TO GREENHOUSES. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED 65 TO 70 MPH. THIS IS AN UPDATE TO PREVIOUS STORM REPORT WHICH REFERENCED BARLOW OREGON.
ODOT REPORTS A LANDSLIDE OCCURRED NEAR MULTNOMAH FALLS ON THE OLD COLUMBIA RIVER HIGHWAY.
ODOT REPORTS LANDSLIDE ON US26 AFFECTING WESTBOUND LANE NEAR MILEPOST 37.
PICTURE OF WATERSPOUT SUBMITTED BY VIEWER. TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
PICTURE OF WATERSPOUT SUBMITTED BY VIEWER. TIME AND LOCATION EDITED FOR ACCURACY BASED ON FEEDBACK FROM VIEWER.
PICTURE RECEIVED OF A WATERSPOUT NEAR THE WILLAPA NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE. TIME CONFIRMED BY WEAK ROTATION ON KLGX RADAR.
CORBETT HIGH SCHOOL. ACTUAL MAX GUST UNKNOWN, AS POWER WENT OUT AT 316 PM.
LANDSLIDE IN THE 2700 BLOCK OF E SALMON RIVER ROAD IN WELCHES HAS CLOSED THE ROAD BETWEEN FAIRWAY AVE AND WELCHES ROAD.
WSDOT REPORTS LANDSLIDE CLOSING BOTH LANES OF SR-4 WEST OF LONGVIEW, NEAR MILEPOST 50.
